game_name: Romance of the Three Kingdoms II

design_philosophy.core_loop: Select scenario and warlord(s), manage provinces and officers through turn-based actions (dueling, diplomacy, resource management), conquer all territories to win
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing territorial expansion, officer loyalty management, and resource allocation across 41 provinces while competing against multiple AI warlords
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command ancient Chinese warlords during the Three Kingdoms period, recreating historical scenarios from the novel while forging your own path to imperial dominance
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Koei's grand strategy design emphasizes emergent political simulation over narrative fidelity—game explicitly diverges from novel events post-scenario, prioritizing sandbox conquest over story railroads. Stat-based officer mechanics and reputation system create asymmetric power dynamics between player factions

difficulty.checkpointing: Scenario-based progression with manual save capability
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects starting scenario (ranging from early chaos to late-game divided empire), chooses controlled warlord(s), and can create custom characters to adjust initial power balance

progression.upgrade_system: Character stat progression via treasures and special items; officer stats (Intelligence 10-100, War Ability 10-100, Charm 10-100) improve task success rates; province development increases land value
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six historical scenarios unlock sequentially or player-selectable; custom character creation available in unoccupied territories; 200+ unique historical characters available for recruitment
replayability_hooks: Six distinct starting scenarios with different initial power distributions
200+ recruitable unique characters with varying stat profiles
Custom character creation system enables personalized campaigns
Multiple warlord selection allows different faction playstyles
Emergent gameplay diverges from novel after scenario setup

world_building_techniques: Historical scenario selection grounded in Romance of the Three Kingdoms novel timeline (AD 189-220)
41 provinces with territorial control reflecting historical Chinese geography
200+ named historical characters with embedded stat-based personalities
Reputation system creates dynamic faction allegiances independent of plot
Officer intelligence affects adviser prediction accuracy, embedding lore into mechanics

faction_indicators: Warlord selection determines faction alignment (Wei, Wu, Shu, or custom)
Officer recruitment reflects loyalty through Charm stat and reputation system
Territory control visually manifests faction power on map
Custom character creation enables player-faction insertion
danger_signals: Rising warlord reputation threatens territorial neighbors
Officer defection risk increases with low loyalty (reputation-dependent)
AI warlords form dynamic alliances based on power distribution
environmental_storytelling: Scenario selection depicts historical power vacuums and allegiances
Province territories reflect historical Three Kingdoms fragmentation
Character stat distributions embed historical reputation (e.g., high Intelligence/War Ability officers)
Messenger interception mechanics suggest political intrigue and espionage culture
